epigraph.text: The chief source of problems is solutions.

follyOfTheDay.headline: HOA Fines Resident $500/Day For Improper Mulch Aesthetic
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A Suburban Minneapolis homeowners association has escalated its decades-long vendetta against a middle-aged accountant over the precise color and depth of his landscaping mulch. After the resident—who works two jobs to meet his mortgage—applied a slightly richer shade of mahogany mulch to his flower beds, the HOA board convened an emergency session to declare it "out of compliance with Section 4.3.2(a) of the Aesthetic Standards Manual."

When the resident politely inquired what shade of brown was actually permissible, the board issued a written response consisting entirely of cross-references to subsections of a 287-page PDF. He has been given 72 hours to remedy the situation or face $500 daily fines. The board president, a retired dental hygienist who has served for eighteen consecutive years despite no formal elections being held, declined to comment.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: Democracy—or at least the suburban version—persists.
